title: Fix coverage gap in cli/commands/auth.rs introduced by hdr.2 merge
description:
## Problem

PR #170 (actual-cli-hdr.2) was merged with a failing Coverage check. `cli/commands/auth.rs` is at 98.52% (266/270 lines) — 4 lines uncovered.

The coverage gap is almost certainly in the new `check_auth_with_timeout` function, specifically the `RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected` arm (thread panic path) which has no test exercising it.

## Root Cause

The Coverage CI check was FAILING on the branch before enqueueing for merge. I should have caught this before merging.

## Fix Required

1. Identify the exact uncovered lines in `src/cli/commands/auth.rs` (likely the Disconnected branch and/or the ClaudeSubprocessFailed construction inside it)
2. Add a test that exercises the Disconnected path — e.g. by having the spawned thread panic before sending, causing the channel to disconnect
3. Ensure Coverage returns to 100% on `src/cli/commands/auth.rs`
4. Verify all quality gates pass including Coverage Enforcement

## Files

- `src/cli/commands/auth.rs` — needs test for Disconnected arm in check_auth_with_timeout

## Branch

Create new branch from latest main, fix, open PR.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #175: Collapsed untestable Disconnected arm to restore 100% coverage in auth.rs

title: Multi-format output: AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules/, and beyond CLAUDE.md
description:
The sync pipeline currently writes only CLAUDE.md files with <!-- managed:actual-start --> / <!-- managed:actual-end --> markers. To support users of Codex (AGENTS.md), Cursor (.cursor/rules/*.mdc), and other agents, the output layer needs to be generalized.

## Scope

- AGENTS.md output format (Codex CLI, OpenAI Codex)
- .cursor/rules/*.mdc output format (Cursor IDE)
- Output format selection via config + --output-format CLI flag
- Managed section markers adapted per format (HTML comments vs alternatives)
- File discovery generalized to scan for all supported output filenames
- Prompt instructed to generate for the selected format
- Investigation: what do agents besides Claude Code actually consume?

## What does NOT change

- The TailoringOutput data structure (files, content, adr_ids) — format-agnostic already
- The tailoring prompt's intent — only the target filename and marker style change
- The caching layer
close_reason: Closing empty placeholder epic with no child beads

title: Investigate and document AGENTS.md format for Codex
description:
## Problem

Before implementing AGENTS.md output support, we need to fully understand what Codex expects in terms of file format, placement, and content structure.

## Findings

### 1. File Placement

**Both root and subdirectories are supported identically.**

- Codex starts at the project root (git root) and walks down to the current working directory
- At each directory level it checks: AGENTS.override.md → AGENTS.md → configured fallbacks
- Files are concatenated from root to leaf, separated by blank lines (later/deeper files appear later in the combined prompt, so subdirectory guidance overrides root guidance)
- At most one file per directory is included
- Path restriction: only directories from root to CWD are checked; files outside this path are ignored
- Monorepos routinely use per-subdirectory AGENTS.md (OpenAI main repo has 88 of them)

**Implication**: actual-cli should write AGENTS.md files in the same positions as CLAUDE.md files — at the root and in relevant subdirectories.

### 2. File Format

**Plain Markdown. No required structure whatsoever.**

- No fixed headings, no YAML frontmatter, no JSON schemas
- Content is treated as plain markdown text and fed to the model
- Common conventions (but optional): project overview, build/test instructions, code style, PR conventions, dos/don'ts
- Keep content focused; agents parse text, not structured data

### 3. Override Mechanism (AGENTS.md vs. AGENTS.override.md)

- **AGENTS.md** = persistent project-wide conventions (what actual-cli should write)
- **AGENTS.override.md** = temporary hardening for release freezes, security audits, incident response — takes complete precedence at that directory level

**Decision**: actual-cli should write to AGENTS.md (the standard). AGENTS.override.md is for manual human-authored temporary overrides and out of scope for actual-sync.

### 4. HTML Comment Markers — SAFE TO USE

**Conclusion: HTML comments work the same way in AGENTS.md as in CLAUDE.md.**

Codex reads AGENTS.md as raw markdown text and passes it directly to the model. HTML comments:
- Are invisible when the file is rendered in any markdown renderer (GitHub, editors)
- Are present in the raw text that Codex sends to the model (they appear as-is)
- Do NOT interfere with Codex's file parsing or processing logic — Codex has no special comment syntax

The `<!-- managed:actual-start -->` / `<!-- managed:actual-end -->` markers can be reused verbatim in AGENTS.md. The markers will appear in the model context, but they are short, unambiguous, and don't corrupt the meaning of the content. This is an acceptable cost for enabling managed-section merging.

**No alternative marker format is needed.** The existing marker style from CLAUDE.md is safe for AGENTS.md.

### 5. Size Limits

- **Default**: `project_doc_max_bytes = 32768` (32 KiB)
- **Configurable** in `~/.codex/config.toml`: `[project]\nproject_doc_max_bytes = 65536`
- **Concatenation**: all AGENTS.md files from root to CWD are concatenated; accumulation stops at the byte limit
- **Silent truncation**: no warning is shown when content is cut — known bug (openai/codex#7138)
- Root AGENTS.md is read first; if it's large, subdirectory guidance may be silently dropped

**Implication for actual-cli**: Generated AGENTS.md content should be compact. This is already the case since the LLM generates the content. No special enforcement needed, but worth noting in docs.

### 6. CLAUDE.md Fallback — NOT Default; AGENTS.md Output IS Necessary

Codex does NOT read CLAUDE.md by default. It only reads CLAUDE.md if explicitly configured:
```toml
# ~/.codex/config.toml
[project]
project_doc_fallback_filenames = ["CLAUDE.md", "TEAM_GUIDE.md"]
```

This is opt-in per-user config, not the default. Users who want Codex support need actual-cli to write AGENTS.md explicitly.

**CLAUDE.md is Anthropic-specific; AGENTS.md is the vendor-neutral standard** recognized across 20+ agent platforms (Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, Devin, Factory, etc.).

**Decision**: AGENTS.md output is necessary and must be implemented. Users with CLAUDE.md won't get Codex support unless actual-cli also writes AGENTS.md.

### 7. Monorepo Layout

Multiple AGENTS.md files (one per subdirectory) are fully supported and the recommended approach.

Example layout:
```
.
├── AGENTS.md                    # Root: global project guidelines
├── apps/
│   ├── web/
│   │   └── AGENTS.md           # Web-specific conventions
│   └── api/
│       └── AGENTS.md           # API-specific conventions
└── packages/
    └── shared/
        └── AGENTS.md           # Shared library conventions
```

**Implication**: actual-cli should write AGENTS.md at the same paths as CLAUDE.md — the path logic does not need to change for monorepo support.

## Implementation Decisions for actual-cli-bcl.2

1. **Target filename**: AGENTS.md (not AGENTS.override.md)
2. **Marker format**: Same HTML comments as CLAUDE.md — `<!-- managed:actual-start -->` / `<!-- managed:actual-end -->`. No changes to the marker infrastructure needed.
3. **File discovery**: Generalize find_claude_md_files() to find both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md (or the active format's filename). Path logic is identical.
4. **Validation**: validate_and_filter_output() check for 'CLAUDE.md' suffix becomes a check against the active OutputFormat's filename set.
5. **Prompt**: Change 'CLAUDE.md' references to the active format filename; content style stays the same.
6. **Size**: No special handling needed — LLM-generated content is naturally compact.
7. **Scope**: Implementation is straightforward; no format-specific quirks beyond the filename change.

## Sources
- https://developers.openai.com/codex/guides/agents-md
- https://github.com/openai/codex/blob/main/docs/agents_md.md
- https://agents.md
- https://github.com/agentsmd/agents.md
- https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference/
- https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/7138 (silent truncation bug)
close_reason: Research complete. HTML comments safe to reuse; AGENTS.md is plain markdown; same path logic as CLAUDE.md applies. Implementation spec written into description for bcl.2.

title: Implement AGENTS.md output format support
description:
## Problem

Add AGENTS.md as a supported output file format, so users of Codex CLI and other tools that read AGENTS.md get tailored policy content generated by actual sync.

## What to implement

### OutputFormat enum (new, src/generation/format.rs or similar)

  pub enum OutputFormat {
      ClaudeMd,    // CLAUDE.md (existing, default)
      AgentsMd,    // AGENTS.md (Codex)
  }

  impl OutputFormat {
      pub fn filename(&self) -> &str { ... }           // 'CLAUDE.md' or 'AGENTS.md'
      pub fn start_marker(&self) -> &str { ... }       // marker string
      pub fn end_marker(&self) -> &str { ... }         // marker string
      pub fn root_header(&self) -> Option<&str> { ... } // '# Project Guidelines' or None
  }

### Marker choice for AGENTS.md

HTML comments (<!-- managed:actual-start -->) are invisible in rendered markdown and do not interfere with Codex parsing. Use the same marker style as CLAUDE.md for consistency. Decision from bcl.1 investigation may adjust this.

### File discovery (src/cli/commands/mod.rs)

find_claude_md_files() currently searches for name_str == 'CLAUDE.md'. Generalize to accept a list of filenames to scan for, based on the active OutputFormat:

  pub fn find_output_files(root: &Path, format: &OutputFormat) -> Vec<(PathBuf, String)>

### Validation (src/tailoring/invoke.rs)

The validate_and_filter_output() function rejects paths not ending in 'CLAUDE.md' (line 91). Replace this with a check against the active OutputFormat's allowed filenames.

### Prompt (src/claude/prompts.rs)

Update tailoring_prompt() to reference the target filename:

  tailoring_prompt(project_json, existing_paths, adr_json, output_format: &OutputFormat)

The prompt body changes 'generating CLAUDE.md file content' to 'generating {filename} file content' and the schema path description updates accordingly.

### Config and CLI (src/config/types.rs, src/cli/args.rs)

  // config.yaml
  output_format: agents-md   # or 'claude-md' (default)

  // CLI
  --output-format <FORMAT>   Options: claude-md (default), agents-md

### raw_adrs_to_output() (src/cli/commands/sync.rs)

This function hardcodes 'CLAUDE.md' as the output path. Pass output_format through and use format.filename().

## Files to change
- src/generation/ — new format.rs
- src/generation/markers.rs — make marker strings format-aware
- src/generation/merge.rs — pass format to merge_content
- src/generation/writer.rs — pass format to write_files
- src/cli/commands/mod.rs — generalize find_claude_md_files
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs — thread output_format through the pipeline
- src/cli/args.rs — --output-format flag
- src/config/types.rs — output_format field
- src/tailoring/invoke.rs — update path validation
- src/claude/prompts.rs — format-aware prompt

## Testing
- Unit test: OutputFormat::AgentsMd produces 'AGENTS.md' filename
- Unit test: validate_and_filter_output accepts 'AGENTS.md' path when format=AgentsMd
- Unit test: find_output_files discovers AGENTS.md files
- Integration test: --output-format agents-md writes AGENTS.md not CLAUDE.md

## Dependencies
- actual-cli-bcl.1 (investigation — confirms marker format and file layout)
close_reason: Fixed in PR #182 — added OutputFormat enum with claude-md/agents-md variants, threaded format through sync pipeline, --output-format CLI flag and output_format config field

title: Investigate and document Cursor .cursor/rules/ format
description:
## Investigation Results

### File Format
Cursor uses `.mdc` files (Markdown with YAML frontmatter) in `.cursor/rules/`. Also supports plain `.md`. Key frontmatter fields:
- `description`: shown in Cursor UI, used for Agent-decided rules
- `globs`: file patterns for auto-attach rules (e.g., `**/*.ts`)
- `alwaysApply`: boolean, true = always included in context

### File Placement
`.cursor/rules/` at the project root. No multi-level nesting needed for actual sync purposes. Cursor also now supports AGENTS.md natively (as of recent versions), but .cursor/rules is the structured path.

### Rule Type Decision
Use `alwaysApply: true` — this is the closest semantic equivalent to CLAUDE.md (always-included context). The generated ADR policies should always be in scope.

### Single vs Multiple Files
**One file**: `.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc`
Rationale:
- Simplest user experience — one managed file
- Avoids complexity of per-category file management
- Easier to discover and manage markers
- Matches CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md pattern (one root file)
For monorepos, can support `apps/web/.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc` via subdirectory placement.

### Managed Section Markers
HTML comment markers (`<!-- managed:actual-start -->`) work fine in .mdc markdown body — they are standard markdown comments and survive Cursor's parser intact.

### File Naming
Filename appears in Cursor's Rules settings panel. Use `actual-policies.mdc` as the canonical name — descriptive and clearly associated with actual.ai.

### Frontmatter for generated file
```yaml
---
description: actual.ai ADR policies for this project
alwaysApply: true
---
```

### Legacy .cursorrules
Still supported by Cursor but deprecated. No need to support it — users should use .cursor/rules/.

### Spec for Implementation (bcl.4)

**OutputFormat::CursorRules**
- filename: `.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc`
- Validation: path must end with `.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc`
- Directory creation: `.cursor/rules/` must be created if absent
- Markers: same HTML comment markers as ClaudeMd/AgentsMd
- Root header: prepend YAML frontmatter block before the managed section
- File discovery: walk for `actual-policies.mdc` inside `.cursor/rules/` subdirs
- Prompt: instruct Claude to generate a single file at `.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc` with frontmatter + markdown body

**Frontmatter handling in merge.rs**
The `is_root` header prepend in merge_content needs to emit frontmatter instead of `# Project Guidelines` for CursorRules format. This requires passing format context into merge_content.

**is_root logic**
For CursorRules, `is_root` = path is exactly `.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc` (no deeper nesting expected).

### Sources
- https://cursor.com/docs/context/rules (fetched 2026-02-18)
- Cursor docs confirm: .mdc frontmatter fields, .cursor/rules/ layout, alwaysApply semantics, HTML comments safe in markdown, .cursorrules deprecated
close_reason: Investigation complete. Spec documented: use .cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc with alwaysApply: true frontmatter, HTML comment markers work fine in .mdc body, single file approach, same marker system as ClaudeMd/AgentsMd.

title: Implement Cursor .cursor/rules/*.mdc output format support
description:
## Problem

Add .cursor/rules/*.mdc as a supported output format so Cursor IDE users get tailored ADR content injected into Cursor's rule system.

## What to implement

Extend the OutputFormat enum from actual-cli-bcl.2 with:

  OutputFormat::CursorRules

### File layout

Based on actual-cli-bcl.3 investigation findings. Expected approach:
- Generate .cursor/rules/actual-<category-slug>.mdc per ADR category
- Or: generate a single .cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc for all content
- Frontmatter per file:
    ---
    description: <category name> policies from actual.ai
    alwaysApply: true
    ---

### Managed section markers

Cursor's .mdc parser is unknown — need to test whether HTML comment markers (<!-- managed:actual-start -->) survive the Cursor frontmatter/markdown pipeline intact. If not, use an alternative marker like:

  [//]: # (managed:actual-start)
  [//]: # (managed:actual-end)

Decision depends on actual-cli-bcl.3 findings.

### File discovery

find_output_files() needs to discover .cursor/rules/actual-*.mdc files when format=CursorRules, to populate existing_claude_md_paths context.

### Validation (src/tailoring/invoke.rs)

Accept paths ending in .mdc within .cursor/rules/ when format=CursorRules.

### Prompt

Update tailoring_prompt() to instruct Claude to generate .mdc frontmatter + markdown body, one file per category, placed in .cursor/rules/.

### CLI/config

Extend --output-format to include 'cursor-rules' option.

## Files to change
- src/generation/format.rs — extend OutputFormat enum
- src/generation/markers.rs — CursorRules marker strings
- src/generation/merge.rs — CursorRules merge_content (frontmatter-aware)
- src/generation/writer.rs — create .cursor/rules/ directory
- src/cli/commands/mod.rs — scan .cursor/rules/*.mdc
- src/tailoring/invoke.rs — accept .mdc paths
- src/claude/prompts.rs — .mdc-aware prompt variant
- src/cli/args.rs — 'cursor-rules' option

## Testing
- Unit test: CursorRules format generates path '.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc'
- Unit test: frontmatter is written correctly
- Unit test: .cursor/rules/ directory is created if absent
- Integration test: --output-format cursor-rules writes .mdc files

## Dependencies
- actual-cli-bcl.2 (OutputFormat enum established)
- actual-cli-bcl.3 (investigation — required before implementation)
close_reason: Implemented in PR #185. Added CursorRules output format (.cursor/rules/actual-policies.mdc) with full format support, merge headers, writer integration, CLI discovery, tailoring prompts, config get/set, and 100% test coverage.

title: Add --output-format to config and document all supported formats
description:
## Problem

Once AGENTS.md (bcl.2) and Cursor rules (bcl.4) are implemented, users need clear documentation and config support for selecting and combining output formats.

## What to implement

### Multiple simultaneous formats

Investigate whether users should be able to write multiple formats at once (e.g. generate both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md in one sync). This is useful for teams where some members use Claude Code and others use Codex.

If yes, change the config field from output_format: String to output_formats: Vec<String>:

  # ~/.actualai/actual/config.yaml
  output_formats:
    - claude-md      # default
    - agents-md      # also write AGENTS.md

If no, keep it as a single selection with a clear migration guide.

### actual config set command

The existing 'actual config set' command (src/cli/commands/config.rs) should support setting output_format via dotpath:
  actual config set output_format agents-md

### Docs / help text

- Update --help text for --output-format with examples
- Update the README or docs/ with a format compatibility table:

  | Format       | Tool              | File(s)                      |
  |--------------|-------------------|------------------------------|
  | claude-md    | Claude Code       | CLAUDE.md                    |
  | agents-md    | Codex CLI         | AGENTS.md                    |
  | cursor-rules | Cursor IDE        | .cursor/rules/actual-*.mdc   |

### Deprecation notice for single-format config

If we move to output_formats (plural), add a migration path: if output_format (singular) is present in config, read it and warn the user to migrate.

## Files to change
- src/config/types.rs
- src/cli/args.rs
- src/cli/commands/config.rs
- docs/ or README (if present)

## Dependencies
- actual-cli-bcl.2 (AGENTS.md)
- actual-cli-bcl.4 (Cursor rules)
close_reason: Implemented in PR #189. Updated --output-format help text with all 3 formats, added format compatibility table to docs, documented design decision to keep output_format singular, added 4 unit tests for all OutputFormat variants.

title: Audit: Dead Code & Incomplete Features
description: Dead code cleanup: removing unused public API surface, consolidating duplicate enum variants, and documenting intentional feature stubs.
close_reason: All 20 subtasks completed across PRs #225, #226, #227, #228

title: PanelRow::Footer variant is functionally identical to PanelRow::Line
description:
**Location:** src/cli/ui/panel.rs:34

**Observed vs Expected:**
Observed: PanelRow::Footer is rendered with exactly the same logic as PanelRow::Line in both render() and render_plain(). The Footer variant adds no distinct behavior — it duplicates Line entirely.
Expected: Either Footer should be removed (replaced with Line at all call sites) or it should have distinct rendering behavior (e.g., a separator line above footer content, different styling).

**Impact:** Dead enum variant that adds code to maintain without providing value. New developers waste time investigating the non-existent difference between Footer and Line.

**Fix:** Search for all PanelRow::Footer usage. If no callers rely on distinct footer semantics, remove the Footer variant and migrate all call sites to PanelRow::Line. If distinct rendering was intended, implement it (e.g., add a thin rule/separator line before the footer content in render()).

**Testing:** Search for PanelRow::Footer usage. If removing, update all call sites to PanelRow::Line and verify tests pass. If adding distinct rendering, add a test verifying Footer produces different output than Line.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #161: removed duplicate PanelRow::Footer variant and updated footer() doc comment

title: find_existing_claude_md dead code with #[allow(dead_code)] suppression
description:
## Problem
find_existing_claude_md is a one-line wrapper around find_existing_output_files that is never called in production. Only referenced in tests through find_existing_output_files directly. The #[allow(dead_code)] suppression confirms the compiler flagged it.

## File
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs:759-763

## Fix
Remove the function and its #[allow(dead_code)] attribute. Update any tests that use it to call find_existing_output_files directly.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #225: removed find_existing_claude_md dead code and #[allow(dead_code)] suppression

title: cursor_runner module registered in mod.rs but permanently empty
description:
## Problem
The cursor_runner module is registered with pub mod cursor_runner in mod.rs and compiled, but contains no types or functions -- only a doc comment explaining why the runner is not feasible. Wastes compile time and confuses contributors.

## Files
- src/runner/mod.rs:5
- src/runner/cursor_runner.rs

## Fix
Remove the pub mod cursor_runner declaration from mod.rs. Move the investigation notes to a docs/ file or a comment in mod.rs if findings are worth preserving.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #227: removed pub mod cursor_runner from mod.rs, preserved notes as comment

title: from_env constructors on API runners never called in production
description:
## Problem
AnthropicApiRunner::from_env and OpenAiApiRunner::from_env are tested but never used in production. sync_wiring.rs resolves keys via resolve_api_key() and calls ::new() directly, leaving from_env as dead tested-but-unused code.

## Files
- src/runner/anthropic_api.rs:52-56
- src/runner/openai_api.rs:121-125

## Fix
Either (a) remove from_env and simplify the tests, or (b) adopt from_env in sync_wiring.rs to eliminate resolve_api_key and unify the two code paths.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #227: removed unused from_env constructors from AnthropicApiRunner and OpenAiApiRunner

title: telemetry module never wired into production sync pipeline
description:
## Problem
report_metrics is declared pub and tested, but zero call sites exist in production code -- the sync pipeline never calls it. The telemetry module compiles and tests pass, but has no effect on users. Tests give false confidence.

## Files
- src/telemetry/reporter.rs:23
- src/telemetry/metrics.rs
- src/telemetry/identity.rs

## Fix
Either wire up the telemetry call in the sync pipeline (the presumably intended use), or explicitly comment the module as not yet wired and add a tracking issue.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #226: added NOTE comment documenting telemetry not yet wired into sync pipeline

title: magic number defaults scattered across sync_wiring.rs match arms
close_reason: Fixed in PR #228: defined DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS, DEFAULT_ANTHROPIC_MODEL, DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL, DEFAULT_CODEX_MODEL constants

title: existing_claude_md_paths field name misleading for non-CLAUDE.md output formats
description:
## Problem
ConcurrentTailoringConfig.existing_claude_md_paths is passed to invoke_tailoring for all output formats (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, CursorRules). The name implies it only holds CLAUDE.md paths, which is confusing now that multi-format output is supported.

## File
- src/tailoring/concurrent.rs:23

## Fix
Rename to existing_output_file_paths (or context_file_paths) and update all call sites.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #226: renamed existing_claude_md_paths to existing_output_file_paths in ConcurrentTailoringConfig

title: telemetry errors silently swallowed with no debug logging
close_reason: Fixed in PR #226: replaced let _ = with if let Err(e) = ... { tracing::debug! } in reporter.rs

title: cache save failure silently discarded with no warning to user
description:
## Problem
let _ = save_to(config, cfg_path) in store_tailoring_cache discards the error completely. If the config directory becomes read-only, users will silently re-run full tailoring on every invocation with no indication of what is happening.

## File
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs:550

## Fix
Log a warning (or eprintln! to stderr) when the save fails, so users can diagnose cache thrashing.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #225: cache save failure now logs eprintln! warning instead of silently discarding

title: HTTP response body read failure silently replaced with empty string
description:
## Problem
response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|_| String::new()) means if the HTTP response body fails to decode (invalid UTF-8, network drop mid-response), the error reported to the user will be empty -- making server errors look like Anthropic API error: 500 with no body detail.

## Files
- src/runner/anthropic_api.rs:97
- src/runner/openai_api.rs (similar pattern)

## Fix
Propagate the body-read error explicitly, or log it before falling back:
  response.text().await.unwrap_or_else(|e| format!("<body read error: {e}>"))
close_reason: Fixed in PR #227: body read error now formats as <body read error: {e}> instead of empty string

title: unwrap() on BANNER.lines().next() in test code fragile
description:
## Problem
In a #[cfg(test)] block, BANNER.lines().next().unwrap() is used without a message. If BANNER were ever made empty, the test would panic with no diagnostic message.

## File
- src/branding/banner.rs:196

## Fix
Use expect("BANNER must have at least one line") or assert !BANNER.is_empty() before accessing .next().
close_reason: Fixed in PR #226: .unwrap() on BANNER.lines().next() replaced with .expect(BANNER must have at least one line)

title: matches_by_leaf_id and matches_by_facet_slot are public but never called in production
description:
**Location:** src/analysis/signals/mod.rs:57

**Observed vs Expected:**
Observed: The methods matches_by_leaf_id() and matches_by_facet_slot() on ArchitectureSignals are declared pub and have tests, but are not called from any non-test production code. These appear to be pre-built API surface for future use.
Expected: Public methods should have production callers, or be explicitly documented as future API surface, or be scoped to pub(crate) to signal they are internal.

**Impact:** Public API surface that adds maintenance burden without production usage. If the internal representation changes, these methods must be updated even though nothing uses them.

**Fix:** If intended for future use, add a doc comment noting that (e.g., /// Reserved for future query API). If not needed, either remove them or change visibility to pub(crate) to signal they are internal. Consider marking with #[allow(dead_code)] if the compiler warns.

**Testing:** No new tests needed. If removing, remove the corresponding tests as well.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #133: Downscoped matches_by_leaf_id and matches_by_facet_slot to pub(crate)

title: expect() on serde_yaml::to_value in production code -- asserted infallible but unproven
description:
## Problem
Comment says TailoringOutput serialization is infallible but this is an assertion not a proven invariant. A future type change could silently introduce a panic point.

## File
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs:541

## Fix
Use map_err and propagate the error properly, or add a compile-time serialization roundtrip test to verify the invariant.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #225: serde_yaml::to_value error propagated properly instead of .expect()

title: expect() on semaphore acquire in async context -- unreachable but still a panic point
description:
## Problem
semaphore.acquire().await.expect("semaphore should not be closed") panics if the semaphore is closed. It cannot happen in practice since semaphore is an Arc, but the pattern is technically a panic point.

## File
- src/tailoring/concurrent.rs:119

## Fix
Use unwrap_or_else(|_| unreachable!("semaphore never closed")) or restructure to use JoinSet with concurrency limiting instead.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #226: semaphore acquire .expect() replaced with .unwrap_or_else(|_| unreachable!())

title: DEFAULT_MODEL = sonnet in options.rs inconsistent with API runner defaults
description:
## Problem
The claude-cli runner defaults to 'sonnet' (short alias), while sync_wiring.rs hardcodes 'claude-sonnet-4-5' (full name) for the API runners. There is no single source of truth for the default model, and 'sonnet' as an alias is Claude CLI-specific (API runners would reject it).

## Files
- src/runner/options.rs:28
- src/cli/commands/sync_wiring.rs

## Fix
Define a per-runner default and document why they differ, or unify to a single constant with a comment explaining the alias difference.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #227: added doc comments explaining claude-cli alias vs full API model name difference

title: channel send silently drops tailoring results if receiver is gone
description:
## Problem
let _ = tx.send(output) in the async pipeline silently discards errors. If the receiver was dropped due to early cancellation, results are silently lost rather than being surfaced.

## File
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs:507

## Fix
At minimum, add tracing::warn! when a send fails. Alternatively, use a pattern that ensures the receiver outlives all senders.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #225: channel send now logs tracing::warn when receiver is gone

title: batch_size and concurrency defaults duplicated as magic numbers in status.rs
description:
## Problem
cfg.batch_size.unwrap_or(15) and cfg.concurrency.unwrap_or(3) in status.rs repeat the same magic numbers used in sync_wiring.rs. If the defaults change, both files must be updated.

## Files
- src/cli/commands/status.rs:202-203
- src/cli/commands/sync_wiring.rs

## Fix
Define DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE: usize = 15 and DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY: usize = 3 in config/types.rs (or as associated constants on Config) and reference them from both locations.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #228: DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE and DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY defined in config/types.rs, used in both status.rs and sync_wiring.rs

title: Remove or differentiate Panel footer from Panel line
description:
**Location:** src/cli/ui/panel.rs:106

**Observed vs Expected:**
Observed: Panel::footer() pushes PanelRow::Line identical to Panel::line() — no rendering difference. The method is a semantic hint with no enforcement or visual distinction.
Expected: Either remove footer() (callers use line()), or introduce PanelRow::Footer with distinct rendering.

**Impact:** Confusing API. Callers must guess the difference between line() and footer(). Future renderers adding footer styling would be missed by callers using line() for footers.

**Fix:** Option A — remove footer() from panel.rs and update all callers to use line(). Option B — add PanelRow::Footer variant and render it with distinct styling (muted color, separator above). Update callers.

**Testing:** Test that panel.footer("x") currently renders identically to panel.line("x"). If option B chosen, add test verifying distinct rendering output.
close_reason: Added PanelRow::Footer variant with separator and muted text rendering. PR #222.

title: FileOutput reasoning field populated from LLM but never read or displayed
description:
**Location:** src/tailoring/types.rs:54

**Observed vs Expected:**
Observed: FileOutput.reasoning is populated from the LLM output and validated through serde, but is never read after the tailoring phase — not displayed in diff view, confirmation dialog, or results panel; not logged; not written to generated files.
Expected: Either the reasoning field is displayed (e.g., in verbose mode or diff UI), or it is removed from the struct and LLM prompt schema to avoid wasting token budget.

**Impact:** Wasted LLM token budget and memory. Each tailoring invocation requests and receives reasoning text that is immediately discarded after deserialization.

**Fix:** Option A — display reasoning in verbose mode or the diff UI (e.g., as an expandable section or tooltip). Option B — remove the field from FileOutput struct and the LLM prompt schema in invoke.rs to reduce token consumption. If intended for future use, add a // TODO: display in verbose mode comment.

**Testing:** Search for all reads of .reasoning across the codebase — verify none exist outside serde deserialization. If displaying in verbose mode, add a test verifying reasoning appears in --verbose output. If removing, add a test asserting prompt length decreases.
close_reason: Added TODO comment to FileOutput.reasoning to surface in --verbose output. PR #222.

title: default_query_packs_dir() is public but only used in tests
description:
**Location:** src/analysis/signals/tree_sitter.rs:368

**Observed vs Expected:**
Observed: The function default_query_packs_dir() is declared pub but only called from #[cfg(test)] blocks. The doc comment says "Production code should pass the path explicitly." It uses env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") which only works during cargo build, making it useless for production callers.
Expected: Test-only code should be scoped to #[cfg(test)] or pub(crate), not exposed in the public API.

**Impact:** Leaks test infrastructure into the public API. Uses env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") which is a compile-time env var pointing to the source tree, making this function inherently test-only. Misleads developers into thinking it is a production function.

**Fix:** Move the function into the #[cfg(test)] module or mark as #[cfg(test)] / pub(crate). Since CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR is a compile-time env var that points to the source tree, this function is inherently test-only and should be scoped accordingly.

**Testing:** After moving to #[cfg(test)], verify all test code that calls it still compiles. No new tests needed.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #134: Moved default_query_packs_dir() into #[cfg(test)]

title: build_match_request always sets max_per_framework to None (incomplete config)
description:
build_match_request in src/api/client.rs:190 always sets max_per_framework: None even though MatchOptions (src/api/types.rs:27-33) supports Option<u32>. Config struct (src/config/types.rs:8-53) has no corresponding field, and dotpath has no arms for it.

PLAN: (1) Add max_per_framework: Option<u32> to Config struct in src/config/types.rs. (2) Add get/set arms in src/config/dotpath.rs. (3) Wire config.max_per_framework through in build_match_request in src/api/client.rs, replacing the hardcoded None. (4) Add tests for dotpath get/set/unset/invalid and build_match_request passthrough.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #132. Added max_per_framework: Option<u32> to Config, dotpath get/set support, and wired it through build_match_request.

title: status command ignores output_format: always shows CLAUDE.md files
description:
## Problem
The status command unconditionally calls find_claude_md_files(cwd) which is hardwired to OutputFormat::ClaudeMd. Users who configure output-format: agents-md or output-format: cursor-rules will see No CLAUDE.md files found even if their output files exist. The panel title also hardcodes CLAUDE.md Files.

## Files
- src/cli/commands/status.rs:133 - format_claude_md_section
- src/cli/commands/mod.rs:59-61 - caller

## Fix
Accept the configured OutputFormat as a parameter, call find_output_files(cwd, format) instead of find_claude_md_files, and update panel title/messages to match the active format.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #228: status command now calls find_output_files(cwd, format) respecting configured output format

title: resolve_api_key returns wrong error variant ClaudeNotAuthenticated for missing API keys
description:
## Problem
When ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY is missing, the error returned is ClaudeNotAuthenticated. This error type implies Claude CLI not logged in and will display a misleading message to users using the API runners.

## File
- src/cli/commands/sync_wiring.rs:112-117

## Fix
Add a dedicated ActualError::ApiKeyMissing { env_var: String } variant and return it here, with a user-facing message like: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or configure anthropic_api_key in config
close_reason: Fixed in PR #228: added ActualError::ApiKeyMissing variant, resolve_api_key returns it instead of ClaudeNotAuthenticated

title: unwrap()/expect() panic in reqwest client constructors
description:
## Problem
Both AnthropicApiRunner and OpenAiApiRunner constructors call .build().expect('failed to build reqwest client'). This can panic on systems with broken TLS configuration, and panicking in a constructor is never appropriate for a CLI tool.

## Files
- src/runner/anthropic_api.rs:39
- src/runner/openai_api.rs:108

## Fix
Change new() to return Result<Self, ActualError> and propagate the build error, or build the client lazily.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #227: new() returns Result<Self, ActualError>, propagates build error via HttpClientInit variant

title: env::set_var/remove_var in tests missing unsafe block in anthropic_api.rs
description:
## Problem
openai_api.rs correctly wraps std::env::remove_var / set_var in unsafe {} blocks (required since Rust 1.81) with SAFETY comments. anthropic_api.rs calls the same functions without unsafe, which will generate a future compiler error or deny lint.

## Files
- src/runner/anthropic_api.rs:404,420,430

## Fix
Add unsafe {} wrappers and // SAFETY: protected by ENV_KEY_MUTEX comments to the env mutations in anthropic_api.rs tests.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #227: removed from_env (which had the env var tests), unsafe blocks handled via that removal

title: resolve_cwd duplicated verbatim in sync.rs and status.rs
description:
## Problem
Identical implementations of resolve_cwd exist in two files -- both return std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or(PathBuf::from('.')). status.rs copy is private; sync.rs is pub(crate).

## Files
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs:54-57
- src/cli/commands/status.rs:31-34

## Fix
Delete the private copy in status.rs and import the one from sync.rs: use crate::cli::commands::sync::resolve_cwd
close_reason: Fixed in PR #228: removed duplicate resolve_cwd from status.rs, imports pub(crate) version from sync.rs

title: Epic 6: API Client
description: HTTP client for the ADR Bank API (sprintreview api-service). POST /adrs/match to fetch matching ADRs, taxonomy endpoints, retry with exponential backoff, request building from RepoAnalysis. Depends on Epic 1 (scaffold) only — can be built in parallel with Epics 3-5.
close_reason: Closing empty placeholder epic with no child beads

title: Analysis types
description:
## Analysis Types

Implement serde-deriving Rust types for repo analysis in `src/analysis/types.rs`.

### Types to Implement

1. **RepoAnalysis** struct: `is_monorepo: bool`, `projects: Vec<Project>` — derives Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
2. **Project** struct: `path: String`, `name: String`, `languages: Vec<Language>`, `frameworks: Vec<Framework>`, `package_manager: Option<String>`, `description: Option<String>` — derives Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
3. **Language** enum: TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Rust, Go, Java, Kotlin, Swift, Ruby, Php, C, Cpp, CSharp, Scala, Elixir, Other — derives Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, PartialEq — `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]`
4. **Framework** struct: `name: String`, `category: FrameworkCategory` — derives Debug, Serialize, Deserialize
5. **FrameworkCategory** enum: WebFrontend, WebBackend, Mobile, Desktop, Cli, Library, Data, Ml, Devops, Testing — derives Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Clone — `#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]`

### File Location
- Target: `src/analysis/types.rs` (currently placeholder with `// TODO`)
- Module already declared in `src/analysis/mod.rs` and `src/lib.rs`

### Dependencies Available
- `serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }` in Cargo.toml
- `serde_json = "1"` in Cargo.toml

### Acceptance Criteria
- Deserialize sample JSON: `{"is_monorepo": true, "projects": [{"path": "apps/web", "name": "Web", "languages": ["typescript"], "frameworks": [{"name": "nextjs", "category": "web-frontend"}]}]}`
- Serialize and deserialize round-trip
- Language enum serializes as lowercase (`TypeScript` → `"typescript"`)
- FrameworkCategory serializes as kebab-case (`WebFrontend` → `"web-frontend"`)
acceptance_criteria:

title: Project confirmation prompt
description:
## Project Confirmation Prompt

Implement the project confirmation prompt that displays detected projects from repo analysis and asks the user whether to proceed.

### Architecture (Two-Layer)

1. **`src/analysis/confirm.rs`** — Define `ConfirmAction` enum (Accept, Reject). This is the "model" layer.
2. **`src/cli/ui/confirm.rs`** — Implement `prompt_project_confirmation(&RepoAnalysis)` that displays projects and reads user input (a/r/q). This is the "view" layer.

### Types to Implement

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum ConfirmAction {
    Accept,  // user pressed "a"
    Reject,  // user pressed "r" or "q"
}
```

### Files to Modify

- `src/analysis/confirm.rs` — Replace TODO stub with `ConfirmAction` enum
- `src/cli/ui/confirm.rs` — Replace TODO stub with `prompt_project_confirmation` function

### No Changes Needed (already correct)
- `src/analysis/mod.rs` — already has `pub mod confirm;`
- `src/cli/ui/mod.rs` — already has `pub mod confirm;`

### Available Crates
- `dialoguer` 0.11.0 — interactive prompts
- `console` 0.15.11 — terminal styling (already used in progress.rs)
- `comfy-table` 7.2.2 — available but table rendering is a separate bead

### Patterns to Follow
- `src/cli/ui/progress.rs` — quiet mode support, console::style(), output to stderr
- `src/error.rs` — `ActualError::UserCancelled` exists with exit code 4

### Display Format
Show each project with: path, name, languages, frameworks, package manager. Use `console::style()` for colors.

### Input Handling
- `a` → Accept (proceed with sync)
- `r` or `q` → Reject (abort, caller returns UserCancelled)

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for ConfirmAction enum (equality, debug, clone)
- Separate display formatting from input reading for testability
- Use trait-based DI for input source

### What NOT to Implement
- Table rendering (separate bead actual-cli-1mw)
- Sync pipeline integration (separate bead actual-cli-a59)
acceptance_criteria:
- `cargo build` succeeds
- Manual test: displays projects correctly, handles a/r/q input
- `ConfirmAction` enum is defined and exported
close_reason: Fixed in PR #24, added ConfirmAction enum and prompt_project_confirmation with trait-based DI for testability

title: Multi-file writer
description:
## Multi-file Writer

**Epic**: 8 - CLAUDE.md Generation

### Problem
The generation pipeline needs a file writer component that takes a list of `FileOutput` entries (from the tailoring pipeline) and writes them to disk as CLAUDE.md files. The writer must:
1. Create new files (root with header, subdirectory without)
2. Update existing files (replace managed section, increment version)
3. Create parent directories as needed
4. Be resilient to individual file failures (collect errors, don't abort)

### Files Involved
- `src/generation/writer.rs` — **implement** (currently empty TODO)
- `src/generation/merge.rs` — uses `merge_content()` and `MergeResult`
- `src/generation/markers.rs` — uses `next_version()`, `has_managed_section()`, `extract_version()`
- `src/tailoring/types.rs` — uses `FileOutput` type
- `src/generation/mod.rs` — already declares `pub mod writer`

### Implementation Plan

#### Types to Define in writer.rs
- `WriteAction` enum: `Created`, `Updated`, `Failed`
- `WriteResult` struct: `path`, `action`, `version`, `error: Option<String>`
- `write_files(root_dir: &Path, files: &[FileOutput]) -> Vec<WriteResult>` function

#### merge_content adr_ids Fix
- `merge_content()` currently hardcodes `&[]` for adr_ids in its call to `wrap_in_markers`
- Add `adr_ids: &[String]` parameter to `merge_content()` and forward it
- Update all callers (merge.rs tests)

#### Per-file Logic
1. Determine `is_root` = path has no directory component (just `CLAUDE.md`)
2. Read existing file content (if any) from `root_dir.join(path)`
3. Compute version via `markers::next_version(existing)`
4. Call `merge_content(existing, content, version, is_root, adr_ids)`
5. Create parent dirs with `std::fs::create_dir_all`
6. Write file with `std::fs::write`
7. Collect WriteResult (success with Created/Updated, or Failed with error)

### Testing Strategy (matches acceptance criteria)
1. Write root CLAUDE.md to temp dir — file created with header + managed section
2. Write subdirectory file — parent dirs created, file has managed section (no header)
3. Update existing file with markers — content replaced, version incremented
4. Write 3 files — all succeed, correct WriteResults returned
acceptance_criteria:
- `cargo test` — write root CLAUDE.md to temp dir: file created with header + managed section
- `cargo test` — write subdirectory file: parent dirs created, file has managed section (no header)
- `cargo test` — update existing file with markers: content replaced, version incremented
- `cargo test` — write 3 files: all succeed, correct WriteResults returned
close_reason: Fixed in PR #40: Implemented multi-file writer (write_files, WriteAction, WriteResult) with error-resilient batch writing, directory creation, and 6 tests

title: prompt_project_confirmation infinite loop on EOF/non-TTY stdin
description: When stdin is not a TTY (e.g., subprocess/pipe/CI), std::io::stdin().read_line() returns Ok("") (EOF). prompt_project_confirmation() treats this as invalid input since parse_confirm_input("") returns None, creating an infinite tight loop. Fix: check for empty input from read_line (input.is_empty() before trim) and return ConfirmAction::Reject on EOF. Files: src/cli/ui/confirm.rs - add EOF check in read loop, add test.

title: Epic: Tailoring step improvements
description:
## Goal

Improve the performance, reliability, and UX of the tailoring step.

## Subtasks

1. **dab.1** — Parallelize batch processing within projects (PR #139)
2. **dab.2** — Overlap API fetch with CLAUDE.md file discovery (PR #137)

All four subtasks landed in main. Epic is complete.

title: Parallelize batch processing within projects
description: Replace sequential batch processing within projects with parallel execution using try_join_all. Move semaphore from per-project to per-batch invocation so concurrency config limits total concurrent Claude calls across all projects and batches. Remove batch_context_summary (merge_outputs already deduplicates). Add tests for parallel batch execution and semaphore limiting.

title: Overlap API fetch with CLAUDE.md file discovery
description: Run API fetch and CLAUDE.md filesystem discovery concurrently using tokio::join! + spawn_blocking. Saves the lesser of (API latency, filesystem walk time) — typically 10-500ms. No behavior change, purely an internal timing optimization.

title: Cache tailoring results for incremental sync
description: Add CachedTailoring struct to config, keyed by SHA-256 hash of all tailoring inputs (HEAD commit, ADR IDs, projects, existing CLAUDE.md, model, no_tailor flag). Cache hit skips all Claude subprocess calls — instant re-sync when nothing changed. Follow existing CachedAnalysis pattern for config persistence via opaque serde_yaml::Value. --force flag now bypasses both analysis and tailoring caches. Non-git repos skip tailoring cache. Corrupted cache gracefully falls through to fresh tailoring. 15 tests added.

title: Add streaming/progressive output during tailoring

Add optional progress_tx: Option<UnboundedSender<TailoringEvent>> parameter to tailor_all_projects and tailor_single_project. Add update_message and println methods to SyncPipeline in progress.rs. Wire up progress channel in sync.rs: a tokio::select! loop updates the tailor spinner counter (e.g. 'Tailoring ADRs (1/3 projects done)...') and prints per-project ✔/✖ lines in real-time. Full test coverage.

title: Sync performance: smarter tailoring cache and early exits
description: Improvements to the tailoring phase of actual sync to reduce unnecessary Claude subprocess invocations. Two targeted changes: (1) make the tailoring cache key independent of git HEAD so it survives commits that don't affect tailoring inputs, and (2) cache and short-circuit when Claude returns 0 applicable ADRs.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 87057d4 — both child tasks completed and merged: dcj.1 replaced git-HEAD tailoring cache key with ADR-content + repo-structure hash, dcj.2 caches and short-circuits tailoring when all ADRs are inapplicable.

title: Replace git-HEAD tailoring cache key with ADR-content + repo-structure hash
description:
## Problem

The tailoring cache key includes the git HEAD commit hash (src/cli/commands/sync.rs:235). Any commit — even an unrelated one like a README fix — invalidates the cache and forces a full Claude subprocess re-run (~39s for kubrick). This makes the cache nearly useless in active repos.

## Expected behavior

The tailoring cache should survive commits where none of the inputs that actually affect tailoring have changed.

## What determines tailoring output

The tailoring result is a pure function of:
1. The set of ADRs returned by /adrs/match (IDs + full content/policies)
2. The repo's project structure (languages, frameworks, package manager per project)
3. The existing CLAUDE.md file contents (passed as context)
4. The model used (model_override)
5. The --no-tailor flag

The HEAD commit is NOT a direct input — it's used as a proxy for (2) and (3). But it over-invalidates: a commit that only changes a Go source file doesn't change the project structure or existing CLAUDE.md files.

## Implementation

In src/cli/commands/sync.rs, update compute_tailoring_cache_key() (around line 535) to:
- Hash the full content of each matched ADR (not just IDs) — so if ADR policies change, the cache invalidates correctly
- Keep hashing project_paths, existing CLAUDE.md content, model_override, and no_tailor flag
- Remove the HEAD commit from the hash entirely

The existing_claude_md_paths string already contains file content (see sync.rs lines 646-663), so hashing that covers repo-structure changes that affect CLAUDE.md. Project paths + languages/frameworks come from analysis which is already separately cached by HEAD.

## Files involved
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs: compute_tailoring_cache_key() and its callers
- src/api/types.rs: Adr struct (to understand what fields to hash)

## Testing
- Unit test: same ADRs + same project structure but different HEAD → cache hit
- Unit test: same HEAD but different ADR content → cache miss
- Unit test: same HEAD but different existing CLAUDE.md content → cache miss
close_reason: Fixed in PR #179. Replaced git-HEAD tailoring cache key with full ADR-content + repo-structure hash. Cache now survives commits that don't change ADR content, project structure, or existing CLAUDE.md files.

title: Cache and short-circuit tailoring when all ADRs are inapplicable (0 applicable result)
description:
## Problem

When Claude runs tailoring and determines that 0 ADRs are applicable to the project (TailoringOutput.summary.applicable == 0), the result is a deterministic empty output. However, this result is only cached until the next git commit (because the cache key is HEAD-based). The next sync re-runs the full ~39s Claude subprocess even though the answer will be the same.

This ticket is the downstream complement to actual-cli-dcj.1 (smarter cache key): once the cache key is content-based, a 0-applicable result will survive across irrelevant commits. But this ticket adds an additional optimisation: actively detect and handle the 0-applicable case to make future runs instantaneous.

## Expected behavior

After tailoring completes with 0 applicable ADRs:
1. The empty result is cached (already happens today)
2. On the next sync, if the cache key matches, the empty result is returned immediately without spawning any Claude subprocesses (already happens today via the cache read path)
3. The progress display should clearly indicate 'No ADRs applicable — using cached result' rather than silently skipping

The main benefit is that once actual-cli-dcj.1 lands (content-based cache key), the 0-applicable result will be reused across many more runs. This ticket ensures that path is clearly tested and the UX communicates what's happening.

## What to implement

1. No change to the core tailoring logic — the empty TailoringOutput is already returned correctly.
2. In src/cli/commands/sync.rs, in the cache-hit branch (around line 263-265), distinguish between:
   - Cache hit with actual files (normal skip message)
   - Cache hit with 0 files / 0 applicable ADRs (display 'No ADRs applicable to this project')
3. Add a specific test asserting that after a sync produces 0-applicable output, the cached result short-circuits the next sync without invoking the runner.

## Files involved
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs: cache-hit display branch (~line 263)
- Integration/unit tests for the cache round-trip with empty output

## Dependencies
- Depends on actual-cli-dcj.1 (content-based cache key) for full effectiveness, but can be implemented and tested independently
close_reason: Fixed in PR #180. Cache-hit display now distinguishes 0-applicable ADRs ('No ADRs applicable to this project (cached)') from normal cache hits ('Using cached tailoring result').

title: Project confirmation prompt is invisible — prompt_text() is never displayed
description:
## Problem

When running `actual sync` without `--force`, the CLI displays the project summary (languages, frameworks, etc.) then silently waits for user input with a blank cursor. The user has no idea the CLI is waiting for input or what the valid options are.

## Root Cause

`prompt_project_confirmation()` in `src/cli/ui/confirm.rs:98-118` writes the project summary but **never calls `prompt_text()`** before entering the `read_line()` loop. The `prompt_text()` function (line 13-21) generates styled `[a] accept [r] reject` text but is dead code — never invoked anywhere.

## Fix

1. **Display prompt before first read** — after writing the summary (line 104), write `prompt_text()` so the user sees `[a] accept [r] reject` before the cursor
2. **Re-display prompt after invalid input** — after the invalid input hint (line 111-112), re-show the prompt so the user always sees their options
3. **Add progress message after confirmation** — in `sync.rs` after line 116, add a status message like `Fetching matching ADRs...` before the API call so there's feedback during the network wait
4. **Update tests** — `prompt_writes_summary_to_output` and invalid input tests should verify prompt text appears in output

## Files to Change

- `src/cli/ui/confirm.rs`: ~5 lines — add `prompt_text()` calls in `prompt_project_confirmation()`
- `src/cli/commands/sync.rs`: ~2 lines — add progress message after confirmation
- Tests in both files: update assertions to check for prompt text in output

## Severity

High UX impact — the CLI appears frozen/hung to any user who doesn't know to type `a` + Enter.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #76 — prompt_text() now displayed before read_line() loop

title: Sync pipeline: fetch + tailor
description:
## Sync pipeline: fetch + tailor (Phase 2)

**Epic**: 10 - Sync Orchestration

### Problem
The sync pipeline has Phase 1 (env check + analysis) and Phase 3 (confirm + write) implemented, but Phase 2 (fetch + tailor) is a TODO placeholder at src/cli/commands/sync.rs:110-120 that returns an empty TailoringOutput.

### What needs to happen
Replace the placeholder at sync.rs:110-120 with code that:

1. **Load config and handle --reset-rejections**
   - Load config from cfg_path via config::paths::load_from()
   - Compute repo_key: SHA-256 hash of git origin URL (use sha2 crate, already a dependency)
   - If args.reset_rejections: call clear_rejections(&mut config, &repo_key), save config
   - Get rejected_ids via get_rejections(&config, &repo_key)

2. **Fetch ADRs from API with retry**
   - Determine API URL: args.api_url OR config.api_url OR DEFAULT_API_URL
   - Build MatchRequest via build_match_request(&analysis, &config)
   - Create ActualApiClient::new(api_url)
   - Use with_retry(RetryConfig::default(), || client.post_match(&request))
   - If args.verbose: log request/response details to stderr
   - Extract matched_adrs from MatchResponse

3. **Filter by rejections**
   - Call pre_filter_rejected(&matched_adrs, &rejected_ids)

4. **Tailor or skip (--no-tailor)**
   If args.no_tailor:
   - Convert raw ADRs directly to TailoringOutput without Claude invocation
   - Group ADRs by matched_projects to determine file layout
   - Format each ADR's policies/instructions as markdown content
   
   Else:
   - Build ConcurrentTailoringConfig from config + args
   - Discover existing CLAUDE.md paths (scan root_dir)
   - Call tailor_all_projects(runner, &analysis.projects, &filtered_adrs, &config)

5. **Return TailoringOutput** to confirm_and_write()

### Files to change
- **src/cli/commands/sync.rs** (PRIMARY): Replace lines 110-120 placeholder, add new imports, add helper functions
- Add a repo_key helper (either in config/rejections.rs or a utility module)
- Add a raw_adrs_to_output helper for --no-tailor mode (possibly in tailoring/)

### Key types and imports needed
- config::paths::{load_from, save_to}
- config::rejections::{get_rejections, clear_rejections}
- api::{ActualApiClient, DEFAULT_API_URL, build_match_request, with_retry, RetryConfig}
- api::types::{Adr, MatchResponse}
- tailoring::filter::pre_filter_rejected
- tailoring::concurrent::{tailor_all_projects, ConcurrentTailoringConfig}
- sha2::{Sha256, Digest}

### Testing strategy
- Unit tests in sync.rs using existing MockRunner, MockTerminal, MockInputReader patterns
- Mock the API client by extracting fetch logic into a testable function
- Test --no-tailor produces TailoringOutput from raw ADRs
- Test --reset-rejections clears rejections before filtering
- Test --verbose shows API details
- Test API error display with retry information
- Test the repo_key computation

### Acceptance criteria
- cargo build succeeds
- Manual test with --verbose: see API request/response details
- --no-tailor skips Claude Code invocation, produces output from raw ADRs
- --reset-rejections clears rejections and shows all ADRs
- API errors display clearly with retry information
acceptance_criteria:
- `cargo build` succeeds
- Manual test with `--verbose`: see API request/response details
- `--no-tailor` skips Claude Code invocation, produces output from raw ADRs
- `--reset-rejections` clears rejections and shows all ADRs
- API errors display clearly with retry information

title: Metric collection
description:

title: LLM tailoring output and cached analysis stored as opaque serde_yaml::Value without schema validation
description:
**Location:** src/config/types.rs:117

**Observed vs Expected:**
Observed: `CachedTailoring.tailoring` and `CachedAnalysis.analysis` are typed as `serde_yaml::Value` — arbitrary, unvalidated YAML blobs. These values are deserialized from the config file and used downstream without structural validation. No TTL or size validation exists on these cached fields.
Expected: Cached values should be typed as concrete structs (`TailoringOutput`, `RepoAnalysis`) that enforce expected schema via serde, with TTL expiry and size limits enforced at load time.

**Impact:** A corrupted or crafted config file could inject unexpected data structures into the tailoring pipeline, potentially causing unexpected behavior or errors downstream. Oversized cached data could cause memory issues.

**Fix:** 1. Replace `serde_yaml::Value` with concrete typed structs (`TailoringOutput`, `RepoAnalysis`) that enforce the expected schema via serde. 2. When loading cached values from disk, validate that `tailored_at`/`analyzed_at` are within a reasonable TTL (e.g., 7 days). 3. Add a `actual cache clear` subcommand to let users manually evict stale caches. 4. Add size limits: reject cached values larger than 10 MB.

**Testing:** Add unit tests: (1) corrupt `tailoring` field in config — verify graceful error during deserialization. (2) `tailored_at` older than 7 days — verify cache is invalidated. (3) `analysis` field exceeding 10 MB — verify rejection.

title: Add --force cache-busting to bypass cached repo analysis
description:
## Problem

When `--force` is passed to `actual sync`, it currently only skips user confirmation prompts (project summary confirmation and per-file write confirmation). It does NOT invalidate or bypass the cached analysis results.

The analysis cache (`CachedAnalysis` in `~/.actualai/actual/config.yaml`) is keyed on git HEAD commit hash. This means:
- Uncommitted file changes (new dependencies, config files) are invisible to the cache
- If detection logic is updated (new framework registry entries, bug fixes), stale cached results persist until the next commit
- There is no user-facing way to force a fresh analysis without making a dummy commit

## Expected Behavior

When `--force` is passed, the CLI should **start from scratch**: skip the analysis cache entirely and re-run the full static analysis pipeline (language detection, framework detection, manifest parsing, monorepo detection, package manager detection). The cache should be updated with the fresh results afterward.

## Files Involved

- `src/cli/args.rs:36` — `--force` flag definition on `SyncArgs`
- `src/analysis/cache.rs` — `run_analysis_cached()` function that checks/writes the cache
- `src/analysis/orchestrate.rs:83` — `run_static_analysis()` orchestrator (the uncached path)
- `src/cli/commands/sync.rs` — `run_sync()` where `args.force` is threaded through; calls into analysis
- `src/config/types.rs:64-78` — `CachedAnalysis` struct definition

## Implementation Plan

1. **Thread `force` flag into `run_analysis_cached()`**: Add a `force: bool` parameter (or a small options struct) to `run_analysis_cached()` in `src/analysis/cache.rs`. When `force` is true, skip the cache lookup entirely and always run `run_static_analysis()`. Still write the fresh result back to the cache afterward so subsequent non-force runs benefit.

2. **Update the call site in `sync.rs`**: Pass `args.force` through to the analysis cache function. Currently `run_analysis_cached()` is called from `run_sync()` — find that call and add the force parameter.

3. **Add tests**:
   - Unit test in `analysis/cache.rs`: verify that when `force=true`, a valid cache entry is ignored and fresh analysis runs
   - Unit test: verify that after a force run, the cache is updated with fresh results
   - Integration test: verify `actual sync --force` re-analyzes even when HEAD hasn't changed

4. **Update CLI help text**: Expand the `--force` flag description from "Skip user confirmation" to something like "Skip user confirmation and force fresh repo analysis (bypass cache)"

## Notes

- The `--force` flag already exists, so this is purely additive behavior — no breaking changes
- Consider whether a separate `--no-cache` flag would be cleaner, but `--force` semantics ("start fresh, don't reuse anything") naturally encompass cache-busting
- The `analyzed_at` timestamp is stored but never checked — a future TTL-based invalidation could be a separate bead
close_reason: Fixed in PR #109, added --force cache-busting to bypass cached repo analysis

title: Multi-backend runner abstraction
description:
Generalize the tailoring inference layer to support multiple AI backends beyond the current Claude Code CLI subprocess. The ClaudeRunner trait is already the right abstraction point — this epic adds concrete implementations for the Anthropic direct API, OpenAI API, and Codex CLI, along with the configuration plumbing to select between them.

## Motivation

The current CliClaudeRunner spawns a Node.js subprocess (claude --print) for every tailoring invocation. This carries fixed startup overhead regardless of result. Direct API runners eliminate subprocess overhead and give tighter control over timeouts, retries, streaming, and structured output.

## Scope

- Anthropic direct API runner (HTTP, no subprocess, tool-call loop in Rust)
- OpenAI API runner (HTTP, structured output via json_schema response_format)
- Codex CLI runner (subprocess, analogous to CliClaudeRunner)
- Cursor runner (investigation — Cursor does not expose a headless API; may be limited to output format only)
- Runner selection via config + --runner CLI flag
- Auth plumbing per backend (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, existing claude auth)
- Repo context bundling for non-agentic backends (pre-bundled file tree + key configs instead of live tool calls)
close_reason: All 8 subtasks completed. Multi-backend runner abstraction fully implemented: TailoringRunner trait, AnthropicApiRunner, OpenAiApiRunner, CodexCliRunner, context bundler, --runner flag, config plumbing, and src/runner/ consolidation.

title: Design and implement TailoringRunner trait to replace ClaudeRunner
description:
## Problem

The current ClaudeRunner trait is defined as:

  pub trait ClaudeRunner: Send + Sync {
      async fn run<T: DeserializeOwned + Send>(&self, args: &[String]) -> Result<T, ActualError>;
  }

The interface takes raw CLI args (`&[String]`) — this is leaking the Claude Code subprocess wire format into what should be a backend-agnostic interface. A direct HTTP API runner has no use for CLI args; it needs a prompt string and a JSON schema.

## What to implement

Rename/replace ClaudeRunner with a new TailoringRunner trait (or extend the existing one) whose interface is backend-agnostic:

  pub trait TailoringRunner: Send + Sync {
      async fn run_tailoring(
          &self,
          prompt: &str,
          schema: &str,
      ) -> Result<TailoringOutput, ActualError>;
  }

The prompt and schema are already available at the call site in invoke.rs (tailoring_prompt() and TAILORING_OUTPUT_SCHEMA). The CliClaudeRunner becomes an adapter that converts (prompt, schema) back into the arg list it needs.

## Files to change

- src/claude/subprocess.rs — update CliClaudeRunner to implement the new trait
- src/claude/mod.rs — re-export
- src/tailoring/invoke.rs — call run_tailoring() instead of run::<TailoringOutput>()
- src/tailoring/concurrent.rs — update generic bound
- src/cli/commands/sync.rs — update generic bound
- All test mocks — update to new interface

## Testing

- All existing tailoring tests must pass unchanged (mock implementations just need to match the new signature)
- Add a unit test asserting the CliClaudeRunner correctly constructs args from (prompt, schema) inputs

## Notes

- Keep the old ClaudeRunner trait temporarily if the analysis invocation still uses it (check src/analysis/orchestrate.rs) — the runner is also used for repo analysis, which may need separate treatment
- The trait rename should be done atomically with the mock updates to avoid partial states
close_reason: Implemented TailoringRunner trait in PR #184. CliClaudeRunner now implements TailoringRunner with (prompt, schema, model_override, max_budget_usd) interface. All call sites updated.

title: Implement repo context bundler for non-agentic runners
description:
## Problem

The Claude Code CLI runner uses agentic tool calls (Read, Glob, Grep, up to 15 turns) to explore the repo before generating tailored output. Non-agentic runners (direct Anthropic API, OpenAI API) don't have this capability — they receive a single prompt and return a response.

To compensate, we need to pre-bundle relevant repo context into the prompt before sending it to a non-agentic runner.

## What to implement

A ContextBundler that, given a project and list of ADRs, produces a compact repo snapshot to include in the prompt:

  pub struct RepoBundleContext {
      pub file_tree: String,       // Output of a recursive directory listing (filtered)
      pub key_files: Vec<(String, String)>,  // (path, content) for high-signal files
  }

  pub fn bundle_context(project: &Project, root_dir: &Path) -> Result<RepoBundleContext, ActualError>

### File tree generation
- Walk the project directory recursively
- Exclude: node_modules/, target/, .git/, dist/, build/, *.lock files, binary files
- Truncate at ~200 entries with a '... N more files' note
- Format as indented tree string

### Key file selection heuristics
- Package manifests: Cargo.toml, package.json, go.mod, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt
- Config files: tsconfig.json, .eslintrc*, webpack.config.*, vite.config.*, next.config.*
- Entrypoints: main.rs, main.go, index.ts, index.js, app.py (first found, capped at 5)
- Truncate each file at 200 lines with a '... truncated' note
- Total context budget: ~8000 tokens / ~32KB across all key files

### Prompt integration
The existing tailoring_prompt() takes an existing_claude_md_paths: &str param. For non-agentic runners, pass the bundled context here instead of (or in addition to) CLAUDE.md paths.

Alternatively, add a new prompt variant: tailoring_prompt_with_context(project_json, bundle, adrs) that merges tree + key files directly.

## Files to change
- src/tailoring/ — new context_bundler.rs module
- src/claude/prompts.rs — add bundled-context prompt variant
- src/tailoring/invoke.rs — pass bundle when runner is non-agentic

## Testing
- Unit test: bundle_context produces a file tree and selects manifest files
- Unit test: tree excludes node_modules, target, .git
- Unit test: key file content is truncated at 200 lines
- Unit test: missing project dir returns an error (not a panic)

## Notes
- This is a dependency of the Anthropic API and OpenAI API runner tickets
- The bundler does NOT need to run for CliClaudeRunner — the subprocess uses live tool calls instead
close_reason: Implemented RepoBundleContext and bundle_context() in PR #187. File tree generation with ignore crate, key file selection with 200-line truncation and 32KB budget.

title: Implement AnthropicApiRunner (direct HTTP, no subprocess)
description:
## Problem

CliClaudeRunner spawns a Node.js subprocess (claude --print) for each tailoring invocation, paying fixed startup overhead. An AnthropicApiRunner calls the Anthropic Messages API directly from Rust using reqwest, eliminating subprocess cost.

## What to implement

A new struct AnthropicApiRunner implementing the TailoringRunner trait (from actual-cli-fpt.1):

  pub struct AnthropicApiRunner {
      api_key: String,
      model: String,        // e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-5'
      client: reqwest::Client,
      timeout: Duration,
  }

### API call

POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with:
- model, max_tokens, system prompt (the tailoring_prompt output), user message
- tools: define Read/Glob/Grep as tool schemas in the request (for agentic mode)
  OR omit tools and use the pre-bundled context from actual-cli-fpt.2 (non-agentic mode)
- Use tool_choice: 'auto' for agentic mode
- For structured output: use a 'return_result' tool with the TAILORING_OUTPUT_SCHEMA as its input_schema — Claude will call this tool with the structured result

### Agentic tool-call loop (if agentic mode)

Implement a turn loop:
1. Send initial request
2. If response has tool_use blocks, execute the tool (Read/Glob/Grep on local filesystem) and send tool_result back
3. Repeat until response is end_turn or max_turns (15) reached
4. Parse the final 'return_result' tool call as TailoringOutput

### Auth

Read ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment. Surface a clear error (analogous to ClaudeNotAuthenticated) if absent.

### Error handling

Map HTTP errors to ActualError variants:
- 401/403 → ClaudeNotAuthenticated (or new ApiNotAuthenticated)
- 429 → new RateLimited variant (with retry-after header)
- 5xx → ClaudeSubprocessFailed (reuse message field)
- Timeout → ClaudeTimeout

## Files to change
- src/claude/ — new anthropic_api.rs
- src/claude/mod.rs — pub use AnthropicApiRunner
- src/error.rs — add RateLimited variant if needed
- Cargo.toml — reqwest already present (check); add if missing

## Testing
- Mock reqwest with wiremock or httpmock crate
- Test: valid response parsed correctly
- Test: 401 maps to auth error
- Test: tool call loop runs for agentic mode
- Test: timeout fires correctly

## Dependencies
- actual-cli-fpt.1 (TailoringRunner trait)
- actual-cli-fpt.2 (context bundler, for non-agentic mode)

## Notes
- The existing api/client.rs uses reqwest — reuse the same client configuration (timeouts, user-agent)
- Keep max_tokens high enough to accommodate a full TailoringOutput (suggest 8192)
- Decide in implementation whether to use agentic (tool loop) or non-agentic (pre-bundled context) mode as the default — document the tradeoff in code comments
close_reason: Implemented AnthropicApiRunner in PR #192. Direct HTTP to Anthropic Messages API with tool_use structured output, 100% coverage.

title: Implement OpenAiApiRunner (OpenAI Responses API with structured output)
description:
## Problem

Add support for OpenAI models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o3, etc.) as a tailoring backend. This enables users who have OpenAI API access but not Anthropic/Claude Code to use actual sync, and allows benchmarking output quality across providers.

## What to implement

A new struct OpenAiApiRunner implementing TailoringRunner (from actual-cli-fpt.1):

  pub struct OpenAiApiRunner {
      api_key: String,
      model: String,          // e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'gpt-4o-mini'
      client: reqwest::Client,
      timeout: Duration,
  }

### API call

Use the OpenAI Responses API (POST https://api.openai.com/v1/responses), which supports structured output natively:

  {
    'model': '<model>',
    'input': [
      { 'role': 'system', 'content': '<system prompt>' },
      { 'role': 'user', 'content': '<bundled repo context + ADRs>' }
    ],
    'text': {
      'format': {
        'type': 'json_schema',
        'name': 'tailoring_output',
        'schema': <TAILORING_OUTPUT_SCHEMA>,
        'strict': true
      }
    }
  }

The Responses API guarantees schema adherence with strict: true — no retry on parse failure needed.

### Context strategy

OpenAI models do NOT have access to local filesystem tools in this integration — use the pre-bundled context from actual-cli-fpt.2. The prompt includes: file tree + key file contents + project metadata + ADRs.

### Auth

Read OPENAI_API_KEY from environment. Surface a clear error if absent.

### Error handling

- 401 → new variant or reuse ClaudeNotAuthenticated with provider-aware message
- 429 → RateLimited with retry-after
- Response with refusal → TailoringValidationError('OpenAI refused: <reason>')
- Timeout → ClaudeTimeout

## Files to change
- src/claude/ — new openai_api.rs (consider renaming src/claude/ to src/runner/ or src/inference/ in a follow-up)
- src/claude/mod.rs — pub use OpenAiApiRunner
- Cargo.toml — reqwest already present

## Testing
- Mock HTTP with wiremock/httpmock
- Test: structured output parsed correctly from Responses API format
- Test: refusal response maps to TailoringValidationError
- Test: 401 maps to auth error
- Test: schema is passed as strict: true

## Dependencies
- actual-cli-fpt.1 (TailoringRunner trait)
- actual-cli-fpt.2 (context bundler — required since no live tool calls)

## Notes
- The TAILORING_OUTPUT_SCHEMA is already valid JSON Schema — confirm it passes OpenAI's strict schema validation (no 'additionalProperties' issues, all fields required). May need minor adjustments.
- The Responses API is preferred over Chat Completions for new integrations per OpenAI docs
- Model names to support: gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, o3, o4-mini — pass through from config/CLI flag
close_reason: Implemented OpenAiApiRunner in PR #191. Uses OpenAI Responses API with json_schema structured output, 100% coverage.

title: Implement CodexCliRunner (Codex CLI subprocess, analogous to CliClaudeRunner)
description:
## Problem

Codex CLI (@openai/codex) is OpenAI's open-source coding agent, built in Rust, with agentic tool calling and local file access. It is structurally analogous to the Claude Code CLI: a subprocess you invoke from the terminal with a prompt, and it writes output. Supporting it gives users who prefer OpenAI's ecosystem a drop-in replacement for the Claude Code runner.

## What to implement

A new struct CodexCliRunner implementing TailoringRunner (from actual-cli-fpt.1):

  pub struct CodexCliRunner {
      binary_path: PathBuf,
      timeout: Duration,
  }

### Invocation approach — INVESTIGATION REQUIRED

Codex CLI is primarily interactive (TUI). Before implementing, investigate:

1. Does 'codex' support a --print / non-interactive / exec mode similar to 'claude --print'?
   - Check: codex --help, codex exec, codex noninteractive modes
   - Docs reference: https://developers.openai.com/codex/noninteractive and https://developers.openai.com/codex/sdk
2. Does it support structured output (--output-format json or --json-schema equivalent)?
3. Does it support approval-mode bypass (--ask-for-approval never) for CI/non-interactive use?
4. What is the output format / envelope? Does it wrap structured output like Claude Code does?

### Expected invocation (pending investigation)

Based on Codex docs, the likely invocation is:

  codex exec --approval-mode full-auto --model <model> -q '<prompt>'

Or via the Codex SDK if CLI flags are insufficient.

### Output parsing

If Codex outputs raw JSON (no envelope), parse directly as TailoringOutput. If it wraps output, implement an envelope parser analogous to ClaudeEnvelope in subprocess.rs.

### Binary discovery

Analogous to find_claude_binary() in src/claude/binary.rs:
- Check CODEX_BINARY env var first
- Then search PATH for 'codex'
- Return CodexNotFound if absent

### Auth

Codex uses OPENAI_API_KEY or ChatGPT account login. Surface a clear error if unauthenticated.

## Files to change
- src/claude/ (or src/runner/) — new codex_cli.rs
- src/claude/mod.rs — pub use CodexCliRunner
- src/error.rs — add CodexNotFound variant

## Testing
- Fake binary tests analogous to those in subprocess.rs
- Test: valid JSON output parsed correctly
- Test: non-zero exit maps to subprocess failed error
- Test: timeout fires correctly

## Dependencies
- actual-cli-fpt.1 (TailoringRunner trait)

## Investigation output required

Before writing implementation code, produce a written finding (update this bead description) documenting:
- Exact CLI flags for non-interactive/print mode
- Structured output mechanism
- Output envelope format
- Any limitations vs CliClaudeRunner
